apiKeys_rotateWarningBody: Click the button below to generate a new API key. You'll only see it once, so make sure to copy it.
apiKeys_rotateWarningTitle: Generating a new key will invalidate your old key
apiKeys_saveKeyNowBody: This key will not be shown again. Copy it now and store it securely.
appWizard_assignDescription: Assign hosts to application groups. Services inherit their host's group automatically. Override individual services by tagging them directly.
appWizard_defineGroupsDescription_conver: Application groups are a special kind of tag used to organize services into logical application stacks. Create new groups or convert existing tags below.
appWizard_defineGroupsDescription_create: Application groups are a special kind of tag used to organize services into logical application stacks. Create groups below to get started.
appWizard_membershipFootnote: Application group membership can be changed later in tag settings.
appWizard_mspCallout: Create a group for each client using the "Create Your Own" section below.
appWizard_noGroupsYet: Add at least one application group to continue.
auth_demoModeBody: You're about to log in to a demo account. Use the credentials below to explore Scanopy. Note that some actions are disabled in demo mode, and rate limiting is set to strict.
auth_emailAlreadyInUse: An account with this email already exists.
auth_passwordLoginDisabledNoProviders: Password login is disabled but no SSO providers are configured. Contact your administrator.
auth_passwordUpdatedBody: Your password has been successfully reset. You can now sign in with your new password.
auth_resetLinkSentBody: If an account exists with that email, you'll receive a password reset link shortly. Please check your inbox and spam folder.
auth_resetPasswordInstructions: Enter your email address and we'll send you a link to reset your password.
auth_signUpForUpdates: Keep me posted on Scanopy partnerships, press, and community news
auth_termsAndPrivacy: I agree to the terms and privacy policy
auth_youreInvitedBody: You have been invited to join {orgName} by {invitedBy}. Please sign in or register to continue.
billing_subscriptionDelayed: Subscription is taking longer than expected to activate. Please refresh the page.
common_apiKeyNameHelp: A friendly name to help you identify this key
common_areYouSure: Are you sure you want to proceed?
common_confirmDeleteName: Are you sure you want to delete "{name}"?
common_couldNotLoadUser: Could not load ID for current user
common_credentialDemoReadOnly: Credential settings are read-only in demo mode.
common_expirationNeverHelp: Leave empty for keys that never expire
common_firstDiscoveryEmailHint: We'll email you when your first discovery is complete.
common_networkRequired: At least one network must be selected
common_upgradePlan: Upgrade your plan to access this feature
cookies_analyticsDesc: Help us improve the product by collecting usage data tied to your account, such as feature usage and navigation patterns.
cookies_marketingDesc: Marketing cookies help us understand how you found us and improve our outreach.
cookies_necessaryDesc: Essential cookies required for the website to function. These cannot be disabled.
cookies_preferencesDesc: Manage your cookie preferences below. You can enable or disable different types of cookies. See our privacy policy for more details.
cookies_settingsDesc: We use cookies to improve your experience and analyze site traffic. See our privacy policy for details.
credentials_addInterfaces: Add interfaces to this host to configure per-interface credential scope.
credentials_bulkDeleteConfirm: Delete {count} credential(s)? This action cannot be undone.
credentials_bulkDeleteImpact: These credentials are assigned to {networkCount} network(s) and {hostCount} host(s).
credentials_deleteImpact: This credential is assigned to {networkCount} network(s) and {hostCount} host(s).
credentials_description: Credentials let Scanopy collect additional data from hosts — for example, using an SNMP community string to query devices on 161/udp. After creating a credential, assign it to a network or individual hosts in their respective edit modals.
credentials_docsDockerProxy: Need help configuring a Docker socket proxy? See the %link%.
credentials_docsGuide: Learn about credential types, scopes, and assignment in the %link%.
credentials_docsSnmp: Need help setting up SNMP? See the %link%.
credentials_filePathReadByDaemon: File paths are read by the daemon at scan time — the content is never stored in Scanopy.
credentials_scopeBroadcastTooltip: Can be assigned to a network to try on all hosts
credentials_secretStoredInDatabase: Value is stored in the Scanopy database.
credentials_selectCredentialSubtitle: Select a credential from the list to configure its interface scope.
credentials_subtitle: Manage credentials for network discovery and services.
credentials_typeImmutableWarning: The credential type cannot be changed after creation.
daemonApiKeys_confirmBulkDelete: Are you sure you want to delete {count} Daemon API Keys?
daemonApiKeys_confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete daemon API key "{name}"?
daemonApiKeys_enableApiKeyHelp: Manually enable or disable API Key. Will be auto-disabled if used in a request after expiry date passes.
daemonApiKeys_namePlaceholder: e.g., Production Daemon Key, Terraform Deployment
daemonApiKeys_noApiKeysYet: No Daemon API Keys configured yet
daemonApiKeys_noNetworkAvailable: No network available. Please create a network first.
daemons_advancedTooltip: Configure logging, connection, and performance for the daemon
daemons_config_acceptInvalidScanCertsHel: Accept invalid TLS certificates when scanning endpoints. Enabled by default since scanners probe arbitrary internal services.
daemons_config_allowSelfSignedCertsHelp: Allow self-signed certs for daemon -> server connections
daemons_config_apiKeyHelp: Authentication key for daemon (generated via UI)
daemons_config_bindAddressHelp: IP address to bind daemon to
daemons_config_daemonUrlHelpNoPort: Base URL where server can reach daemon
daemons_config_heartbeatIntervalHelp: Seconds between heartbeat updates to the server
daemons_config_interfacesHelp: Restrict daemon to specific network interface(s). Comma-separated for multiple (e.g., eth0,eth1). Leave empty for all interfaces
daemons_config_logFileHelp: Path to log file. Defaults to platform-specific path. Set to "none" to disable file logging.
daemons_config_modeHelp: DaemonPoll: Daemon connects to server; works behind NAT/firewall without opening ports. ServerPoll: Server connects to daemon, for deployments where daemon cannot make outbound connections - requires providing Daemon URL
daemons_config_namePlaceholder: Enter a name for this daemon...
daemons_config_networkIdHelp: UUID of the network to scan
daemons_config_sectionRuntimeDesc: Logging, heartbeat, and network interface settings
daemons_config_sectionServerConnectionDe: Bind address and certificate settings for daemon connectivity
daemons_config_serverUrlHelp: URL where the daemon can reach the server
daemons_configureReachabilityFailed: Check that the daemon port is open, any firewall allows inbound connections, and port forwarding is configured if the daemon is behind NAT. Configuration varies by platform — consult your system or network administrator if unsure.
daemons_confirmBulkDelete: Are you sure you want to delete {count} Daemons?
daemons_confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete daemon "{name}"?
daemons_credentialWizardDaemonHostHelp: This credential will be tried on the daemon's own host using localhost (127.0.0.1).
daemons_credentialWizardDescription: Add credentials for the daemon to use when scanning. %link%.
daemons_credentialWizardExistingDescript: You can modify this credential in the Credentials tab.
daemons_credentialWizardNetworkCredentia: Will be used for all hosts:
daemons_credentialWizardReturn: Create {count} credential(s) and return to install
daemons_credentialWizardSelectExisting: Select an existing credential to add
daemons_credentialWizardTargetIpHelp: The daemon will use this credential to connect to the service at this IP. If it succeeds, future scans will automatically reuse the credential for this host.
daemons_credentialWizardTooltip: Add credentials for deeper device discovery
daemons_dockerApplyChanges: Then run docker compose up -d to apply the changes.
daemons_dockerDescription: Configure how this daemon discovers Docker containers
daemons_dockerDisabledHelp: Docker container discovery will be skipped
daemons_dockerLatestTag: If you're using the :latest tag, pull the new image and restart:
daemons_dockerLinuxOnlyBody: Docker Compose deployment is only supported on Linux.
daemons_dockerLocalSocketHelp: Daemon will scan its local Docker socket for containers
daemons_dockerPinnedVersion: If you're pinned to a specific version, update the image line in your
daemons_dockerProxyHelp: Configure a Docker API proxy for remote container discovery
daemons_dockerProxyWizardCta: Create a Docker Proxy credential in Scan Credentials
daemons_docsConfigOptions: Need a full reference for all configuration options? See the %link%.
daemons_docsMacvlan: Need network isolation without host networking? See the %link%.
daemons_docsMultiVlan: Need help with your daemon deployment strategy? See the %link%.
daemons_docsPollingMode: Not sure which mode to use? See %link%.
daemons_docsUpgradeMultipleDaemons: See the multi-daemon %link% for more details.
daemons_fixValidationErrorsBody: Correct the field validation issues above before using the installation commands.
daemons_generateNewKeyHelp: Generate a new key if this is a fresh daemon setup.
daemons_httpDaemonUrlWarning: This daemon URL uses HTTP. Credentials will be sent unencrypted. Ensure the connection is secured through other means (e.g., VPN, private network).
daemons_installCommandDescription: This command will download and install the daemon, then start it with your configuration.
daemons_installCommandEmailed: Install command sent to your email
daemons_installCopiedToastDocker: Command copied! Save it as docker-compose.yml and run docker compose up -d, then confirm below.
daemons_installCopiedToastLinux: Command copied! Run it on your server, then confirm you've installed it.
daemons_installCopiedToastMac: Command copied! Run it in Terminal, then confirm you've installed it.
daemons_installCopiedToastWindows: Command copied! Run it in PowerShell, then confirm you've installed it.
daemons_networkCannotChange: Network cannot be changed after API key is generated
daemons_promptBody: The first step to generating your network map is installing a lightweight daemon to scan for hosts, services, and connections. Ready to get started?
daemons_troubleshoot_canReachServer: Can the daemon reach the server?
daemons_troubleshoot_canReachServerStep1: Run this command to test connectivity. If it returns JSON, the server is reachable:
daemons_troubleshoot_canReachServerStep2: If that fails, test DNS resolution. You should see an IP address in the response:
daemons_troubleshoot_canReachServerStep3: If DNS resolves but connectivity still fails, check that your firewall allows outbound traffic on {portDesc}.
daemons_troubleshoot_canServerReach: Can the server reach the daemon?
daemons_troubleshoot_canServerReachDesc: Use the button below to test if the server can connect to your daemon. The daemon must be reachable from the internet (or the server's network).
daemons_troubleshoot_checkLogsDesc: Daemon logs contain detailed error information. Check the log file or use these commands:
daemons_troubleshoot_connectedDesc: Discovery has automatically started. You can view the progress in real time.
daemons_troubleshoot_errApiKeyCause: Key is invalid or was regenerated
daemons_troubleshoot_errApiKeyFix: Re-run the install command from the wizard
daemons_troubleshoot_errConnRefusedCause: Server not running or wrong URL/port
daemons_troubleshoot_errConnRefusedFix: Check server is running; verify URL and port
daemons_troubleshoot_errDnsFix: Check the server URL hostname and DNS configuration
daemons_troubleshoot_errNotScanopyCause: URL points to the wrong service
daemons_troubleshoot_errNotScanopyFix: Verify the server URL is correct
daemons_troubleshoot_errTimeoutCause: Firewall blocking outbound traffic or server unreachable
daemons_troubleshoot_errTlsFix: Enable self-signed certs in the daemon config
daemons_troubleshoot_firewallNatDesc: The server must be able to reach the daemon at {daemonUrl}. If behind NAT, configure port forwarding. Ensure the firewall allows inbound connections on the daemon port.
daemons_troubleshoot_firewallNote: Firewall configuration varies by platform and provider. Consult your system or network administrator if unsure.
daemons_troubleshoot_followSteps: Follow this checklist to diagnose the issue:
daemons_troubleshoot_healthPartial: Port open but health check failed
daemons_troubleshoot_healthPartialDesc: The port is open but the daemon isn't responding to health checks. It may still be starting — try again in a moment.
daemons_troubleshoot_healthReachableDesc: The server should register it on its next polling cycle. If this persists, try restarting the daemon.
daemons_troubleshoot_healthUnreachableDe: The daemon may not be running, or the port may no longer be reachable. Verify the install command completed and the daemon process started.
daemons_troubleshoot_isDaemonRunningDesc: Run this command on the daemon host to check if the process is active:
daemons_troubleshoot_isListening: Is the daemon running and listening?
daemons_troubleshoot_isListeningDesc: Check that the daemon process is running and listening on the configured port:
daemons_troubleshoot_logFileMounted: Or on the host via mounted volume:
daemons_troubleshoot_processNotFound: If the process is not running, go back and re-run the install command.
daemons_troubleshoot_reachablePolling: Daemon is reachable and healthy — the server will register it on its next polling cycle
daemons_troubleshoot_stillStuckDesc: If none of the above resolved the issue, reach out for help:
daemons_troubleshoot_troubleTitle: Your daemon hasn't connected to this server
daemons_troubleshoot_troubleTitleServerP: The server hasn't been able to connect to your daemon
daemons_troubleshoot_waitingDesc: This usually takes less than a minute. Make sure the daemon is running.
daemons_troubleshoot_waitingTitle: Waiting for your daemon to connect to the server...
daemons_updateAvailable: An update is available for daemon
daemons_upgradeConfigPreserved: Your daemon configuration is preserved automatically — no need to re-enter server URL, API key, or other settings.
daemons_upgradeMultipleDaemons: Running multiple daemons on this machine?
daemons_upgradeMultipleDaemonsBody: All daemons share the same binary. After upgrading, restart each one with its name. To list your daemon names, check your config directory:
daemons_useExistingKeyHelp: Use an existing key if your organization manages API keys centrally or you've already generated one.
dependencies_confirmBulkDelete: Are you sure you want to delete {count} Dependencies?
dependencies_dependencyNamePlaceholder: e.g., DNS Resolution Path, Web Access Chain
dependencies_descriptionPlaceholder: Describe the data flow or purpose of this dependency...
dependencies_edgeColorHelp: Choose the color for edges in this dependency
dependencies_edgeStyleHelp: Choose how edges are drawn between services
dependencies_noDependenciesHelp: Dependencies define clusters or paths of services for visualization
dependencies_noServicesYet: No services in this dependency yet.
dependencies_serviceBindingsInfoBody: A binding is a service on a specific host, interface, and port. By ordering bindings, you define the path requests take — which service sends them and which service receives them. For Request Path dependencies, bindings are connected in sequence. For Hub and Spoke, the first binding is the central service.
dependencies_serviceBindingsInfoTitle: Service bindings describe how services communicate across your network.
dependencies_servicesHelp: Select and order the services in this dependency
dependencies_servicesInfoBody: Select the services involved and arrange them in order. Use "With ports" if you want the topology to show which specific ports traffic flows through.
dependencies_subtitle: Track how services depend on each other to understand your application architecture
discovery_adHocDescription: This discovery will only run when manually triggered
discovery_allInterfacedSubnets: All subnets daemon had an interface with at time of scan
discovery_allSubnetsScanned: All subnets that daemon has an interface with at time of scan will be scanned
discovery_arpScanCutoffWarning: The following interfaced subnets will be truncated to /{cutoff} (~{ipCount} IPs): {subnets}.
discovery_arpScanCutoffWarningSlow: The following interfaced subnets will be truncated to /{cutoff} (~{ipCount} IPs): {subnets}. At the default rate of 50 pps, each ARP round would take up to {timeEstimate}. Increase ARP Scan Rate to reduce scan time.
discovery_cannotDeleteWhileRunning: Cannot delete while discovery is running
discovery_cannotToggleWhileRunning: Cannot enable or disable while discovery is running
discovery_confirmDeleteHistorical: Are you sure you want to delete {count} Historical Discoveries?
discovery_confirmDeleteScheduled: Are you sure you want to delete {count} Scheduled Discoveries?
discovery_couldNotGetNetworkId: Could not get network ID from selected daemon. Please try a different daemon.
discovery_credentialsDescription: Add credentials for this discovery to use when scanning. %link%.
discovery_daemonHelp: The daemon that will execute this discovery
discovery_daemonHostMissingHelp: Could not find a host associated to the selected daemon. It may have been deleted or corrupted. Please recreate the daemon.
discovery_dockerSocketInfo: This daemon already has local Docker socket access. This proxy will take priority over the socket.
discovery_dockerSocketInfoUnknown: If this daemon has local Docker socket access, this proxy will take priority over it.
discovery_docsScanSettings: Learn how scan speed settings affect discovery in the %link%.
discovery_editActiveInfo: This discovery is currently running. Changes will take effect on the next scan.
discovery_firstScanMustBeLight: The first discovery scan always uses a light port set. These settings can be configured after the first scan.
discovery_forceFullScan: Force full scan on next run
discovery_forceFullScanHelp: Override the scan interval and run a full 65k port scan on the next discovery run. This flag is cleared after the scan completes.
discovery_hostNameFallbackHelp: In the event that hostname can't be resolved, what name should be set for discovered hosts? IP Address, or best service (the highest confidence service match)?
discovery_legacyDaemonsWarning: Some daemons are running versions prior to v0.15.0 and use legacy discovery types (Network, Docker, Self Report). After upgrading, their discoveries will be automatically consolidated into a single unified discovery per daemon. Schedules and settings will be preserved. Learn more about the migration.
discovery_manualDiscoveryHelp: This discovery will only run when you manually trigger it from the discoveries page. No automatic scheduling is configured.
discovery_namePlaceholder: e.g., Daily Network Scan, Docker Services Check
discovery_noDaemonSelected: No daemon selected; can't set up discovery
discovery_noHistorySessions: No discovery sessions have been run
discovery_noHistorySessionsSubtitle: Once a daemon completes a discovery, results will appear here.
discovery_nonInterfacedSubnetSlow: The selected daemon does not have a direct network interface with the following subnets: {subnets}. Hostnames and MAC addresses will not be available for discovered hosts. The following subnets have a large address space and scanning may take several hours: {largeSubnets}. To scan these subnets more effectively, see the planning daemon deployment guide to deploy a daemon with a direct interface.
discovery_nonInterfacedSubnetWarning: The selected daemon does not have a direct network interface with the following subnets: {subnets}. You can still include them, but hostnames and MAC addresses will not be available for any discovered hosts. To scan these subnets more effectively, see the planning daemon deployment guide to deploy a daemon with a direct interface.
discovery_scanModeBaselinePending: The next scan will be a full 65k port scan to establish a baseline. After that, the full scan interval takes effect.
discovery_scanModeFirstLight: The first scan uses a light port set for faster initial results.
discovery_scanModeIntervalExplainer: The first scan is always light (fast initial results). The second scan is always a full 65k port baseline. After that, full scans run at this interval.
discovery_scanSettingsHelp: Configure scan performance. Default values work well for most networks.
discovery_scheduleCronInfo: This schedule uses a custom cron expression. Click 'Reset to day picker' to use the visual editor.
discovery_scheduleHelp: Configure when this discovery should run automatically
discovery_scheduleTimezoneHelp: Cron schedule is evaluated in this timezone
discovery_scheduledDescription: This discovery will run automatically on a schedule
discovery_targetSubnetsHelp: Select specific subnets to scan, or leave empty to scan all subnets that the daemon has an interface with.
discovery_upgradeConsolidationWarning: This daemon's existing discoveries will be consolidated into a single unified discovery after upgrading to v0.15.0. Schedules and settings will be preserved. Learn more.
discovery_upgradeRequiredBody: This daemon is running a version prior to v0.15.0 and must be upgraded to create new discoveries. Learn more.
errors_auth_daemon_key_not_created: Daemon is trying to register with an API key that has not yet been created
errors_auth_demo_mode: This action is disabled in demo mode
errors_auth_email_verification_required: Please verify your email to access this feature
errors_auth_oidc_not_configured: OIDC not configured for this organization
errors_auth_organization_required: This operation requires an organization context
errors_auth_permission_denied: You don't have permission to perform this action
errors_auth_session_expired: Your session has expired. Please log in again.
errors_billing_feature_not_available: Your current plan does not include {feature}. Upgrade your plan to access this feature.
errors_billing_host_limit_reached: You've reached the limit of {limit} hosts on your current plan. Upgrade for unlimited hosts.
errors_billing_plan_limit_reached: You've reached the limit of {limit} {resource} on your current plan
errors_daemon_identity_mismatch: Cannot send updates for a different daemon
errors_daemon_network_mismatch: Cannot send updates for a different network
errors_daemon_not_registered: Daemon not found on server. It may have been deleted or the database was reset. Reinstall or reconfigure the daemon.
errors_daemon_standby: Your plan does not support DaemonPoll mode. The daemon is on standby. Upgrade your plan and restart the daemon to resume.
errors_daemon_version_too_old: Daemon version {daemon_version} is older than server version {server_version}. Update the daemon to match the server version.
errors_database_duplicate_entry: A record with this {field} already exists
errors_discovery_historical_read_only: Historical discovery cannot be modified via API
errors_discovery_subnet_network_mismatch: Subnet '{subnet}' is on a different network
errors_entity_access_denied: You don't have access to this {entity}
errors_entity_in_use: Cannot delete {entity} '{name}' because it's used by {used_by}
errors_entity_reference_invalid: Referenced {entity} in field '{field}' does not exist
errors_interface_ip_out_of_range: IP address '{ip}' is not within subnet '{subnet}' range
errors_invite_already_accepted: This invite has already been accepted
errors_invite_email_mismatch: Invite email doesn't match your account
errors_networks_access_denied: You don't have access to network '{network}'
errors_rate_limit_exceeded: Too many requests, please try again later
errors_validation_bulk_empty: No IDs provided for bulk operation
errors_validation_max_length: Field '{field}' must be at most {max} characters
errors_validation_min_length: Field '{field}' must be at least {min} characters
home_demoEmbedSubtitle: This is a live demo of Scanopy's interactive topology view — you'll have a visual map of your network like this once your first scan completes.
home_noDiscoveriesYet: No discovery results yet. Set up a daemon to start discovering your network.
home_nudges_scheduledScansDescription: Upgrade to automatically discover network changes on a schedule.
home_nudges_unclaimedPortsDescription: Some hosts have open ports that weren't matched to known services. You can review and reassign them.
hosts_confirmBulkDelete: Are you sure you want to delete {count} Hosts?
hosts_consolidateModal_credentialsMigrat: {count} credential assignment(s) from "{source}" will be migrated to "{destination}"
hosts_consolidateModal_interfacesMigrate: {count} interface(s) from "{source}" will be migrated to "{destination}"
hosts_consolidateModal_portsMigrated: {count} port(s) from "{source}" will be migrated to "{destination}"
hosts_consolidateModal_previewSubtitle: Review the changes before confirming the consolidation.
hosts_consolidateModal_selectHost: Select host which {hostName} will be consolidated with:
hosts_consolidateModal_servicesMigrated: {count} service(s) from "{source}" will be migrated to "{destination}"
hosts_consolidateModal_warningBody: The source host will be permanently deleted and converted to an interface. Make sure this is what you want before proceeding.
hosts_credentialOverrideHelp: Assign credentials here to try on this host, in addition to network defaults. %link%.
hosts_credentialScopeSubtitle: Restrict which interfaces this credential will be tried on. Leave empty to try on all interfaces.
hosts_details_descriptionPlaceholder: Describe what this host does or its role in your infrastructure
hosts_details_namePlaceholder: Enter a name for this host...
hosts_editor_basicInfo: Basic host information and connection details
hosts_editor_virtualizationDesc: VMs and containers managed by services on this host
hosts_ifEntries_emptySubtitle: SNMP interface table entries will appear here after an SNMP-enabled discovery scan finds this host
hosts_ifEntries_selectToView: Select an interface to view details
hosts_ifEntries_subtitle: Entries from host's ifTable collected via SNMP
hosts_interfaces_deleteMessage: This interface has bindings from the following services. Deleting it will remove those bindings.
hosts_interfaces_emptyMessage: No interfaces configured. Add one to get started.
hosts_interfaces_helpText: Configure network interfaces and addresses. Drag to reorder.
hosts_interfaces_macReadOnly: MAC addresses cannot be changed after creation
hosts_interfaces_placeholder: Select subnet to create interface with...
hosts_interfaces_selectToConfig: Select an interface from the list to configure it
hosts_ports_configSubtitle: Configure the port number and protocol
hosts_ports_deleteMessage: This port has bindings from the following services. Deleting it will remove those bindings.
hosts_ports_emptyMessage: No ports on this host. Add one to get started.
hosts_ports_selectToConfig: Select a port from the list to configure it
hosts_ports_wellKnownSubtitle: This is a well-known or registered port, and can't be edited
hosts_services_bindingsHelp: For a given interface, a service can have either port bindings OR an interface binding, not both.
hosts_services_couldNotFindService: Could not find service to create binding for
hosts_services_couldNotFindServiceToRemo: Could not find service to remove binding for
hosts_services_emptyMessage: No services configured yet. Add one to get started.
hosts_services_helpText: Services define what this host provides to the network. The icon for the first service in the list will be used as the host's logo in the Host tab.
hosts_services_interfaceBindingsHelp: Service is present at an interface (IP address) without a specific port
hosts_services_noAvailablePortCombos: No available port+interface combinations to bind
hosts_services_noInterfaces: Host does not have any interfaces, can't create binding
hosts_services_noPorts: Host does not have any ports, can't create binding
hosts_services_portBindingsHelp: Service listens on a specific port, optionally on a specific interface
hosts_services_selectToConfig: Select a service from the list to configure it
hosts_services_unclaimedPortsHint: Unclaimed ports weren't matched to a known service during discovery. %link%
hosts_virtualization_containerHelp: Manage containers controlled by this {serviceName} instance
hosts_virtualization_emptyMessage: No virtualization services on this host.
hosts_virtualization_emptySubtitle: Container and VM relationships will appear here when Docker or hypervisor services are discovered on this host
hosts_virtualization_noContainersYet: No containers managed by this service yet. Add services that run in containers on this host.
hosts_virtualization_noVmsYet: No VMs managed by this service yet. Add hosts that are VMs running on this hypervisor.
hosts_virtualization_pleaseSaveBody: You need to save {name} before managing virtualization
hosts_virtualization_selectToManage: Select a virtualization service from the list to manage its VMs or containers
hosts_virtualization_servicesHelp: Services that manage virtual machines or containers on this host
hosts_virtualization_unknownTypeSubtitle: This service has an unrecognized virtualization management type
hosts_virtualization_vmHelp: Manage VMs controlled by this {serviceName} instance
invites_confirmRevoke: Are you sure you want to revoke this invite URL?
networks_confirmBulkDelete: Are you sure you want to delete {count} Networks?
networks_confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete network "{name}"? All hosts, groups, and subnets will be deleted along with it.
networks_credentialHelp: Assign credentials here to try on all hosts in this network. You can also assign to specific hosts in the host modal. %link%.
onboarding_commercialNoticeBody: Scanopy Community is licensed under AGPL-3.0. For commercial use, please ensure your use complies with the license terms, or consider our commercial self-hosted offering.
onboarding_companyNetworkHelp: Each network represents a scannable environment, like an office or data center. Create additional networks to map multiple locations.
onboarding_freebsdNotSupported: Scanopy's daemon currently doesn't support FreeBSD. We recommend running the daemon on a Linux host or VM on your network instead.
onboarding_homelabDescription: Home network, NAS, Raspberry Pi, smart devices
onboarding_homelabNetworkHelp: Each network represents a scannable environment. Create additional networks to map separate environments.
onboarding_homelabOrgLabel: What should we call your organization?
onboarding_howDidYouHear: How did you hear about Scanopy?
onboarding_mspNetworkHelp: Each network represents a scannable environment, like a customer site. One customer can have multiple networks.
onboarding_openbsdNotSupported: Scanopy's daemon currently doesn't support OpenBSD. We recommend running the daemon on a Linux host or VM on your network instead.
onboarding_orgHelp: Your organization is your account. All networks, hosts, and team members belong to it.
onboarding_otherDescription: Government, nonprofit, education, or something else
onboarding_referralSource_otherPlacehold: Please tell us how you found us...
onboarding_tailorSetup: We'll tailor the setup to your needs
services_confirmBulkDelete: Are you sure you want to delete {count} Services?
services_confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete "{serviceName}"?
services_subtitle: To create a service, add it to a host in the Hosts tab.
settings_account_cookiePreferencesDesc: Manage your cookie and tracking preferences
settings_account_oidcLinkHelp: Link your account with an identity provider for faster sign-in. You can only link one provider at a time.
settings_account_setPassword: Set a password to enable email and password login
settings_account_unlinkFirst: Unlink {provider} first to link this provider
settings_billing_canceled: Your subscription has been canceled. Access will end at the end of your billing period.
settings_billing_changePlanDescription: View available plans and upgrade or downgrade
settings_billing_downgrade_pending: Your plan will change to Free at the end of your current billing cycle. To cancel this change, upgrade to a paid plan or manage your subscription in the billing portal.
settings_billing_pastDue: Your payment is past due. Please update your payment method to continue using Scanopy.
settings_billing_trialActive: Your trial is active. You won't be charged until your trial ends.
settings_billing_upgradePlanDescription: Get scheduled discovery, DaemonPoll mode, and more hosts
settings_org_deleteConfirm: This will permanently delete your organization, all data, and all user accounts. This action cannot be undone.
settings_org_deleteHelp: Permanently delete this organization and all its data. This cannot be undone.
settings_org_deleteTypeName: Type your organization name to confirm
settings_org_populateConfirm: Are you sure you want to populate demo data? This will add sample networks, hosts, and services to your organization.
settings_org_populateDemoHelp: Fill the organization with sample data for demonstration purposes.
settings_org_resetConfirm: Are you sure you want to reset all organization data? This will delete all entities and users from your organization. This action cannot be undone.
settings_org_resetDataHelp: Delete everything except for any organization owner user account. This cannot be undone.
settings_system_themeDesc: Choose how Scanopy looks to you
shares_allowedDomainsHelp: Restrict which domains can embed this share. Leave empty to allow all domains.
shares_cacheInfoBody: Share links and embeds are cached. Any updates you make won't be visible immediately.
shares_cacheInfoTitle: Changes may take up to 5 minutes to appear
shares_confirmBulkDelete: Are you sure you want to delete {count} shares?
shares_createdHelp: To edit this share's settings, go to the Sharing tab.
shares_noSharesSubtitle: Create links or embeds from the Topology tab to share your topologies
shares_noSharesYet: No links or embeds created yet
shares_passwordHelpCreate: Leave empty to allow public access with no password
shares_passwordHelpEdit: Leave empty to keep the current password
shares_upgradeForEmbeds: Upgrade your plan to embed this share on external websites.
snmp_enableForNetwork: Enable SNMP discovery for this network
snmp_hostOverrideBody: SNMP discovery always tries the "public" community string. Custom credentials are tried in addition to "public", not instead of it. You can set a credential on a Network for all hosts, or override for specific hosts after discovery.
subnets_confirmBulkDelete: Are you sure you want to delete {count} Subnets?
subnets_descriptionPlaceholder: Describe the purpose of this subnet...
support_description: Need help with Scanopy? Choose one of the options below to get support.
support_discordDesc: Join our community for help and discussions
support_incorrectDetectionDesc: Report a service that was incorrectly identified
support_reportBugDesc: Found an issue? Let us know so we can fix it
support_requestFeatureDesc: Suggest a new feature or improvement
support_userGuideDesc: Read the full documentation and guides
tags_applicationGroupHelp: Application group tags are used to group services in the topology's Application perspective. Each host or service can have one application group tag. Services inherit their host's application group unless overridden.
tags_confirmBulkDelete: Are you sure you want to delete {count} tags?
tags_crossGroupSelectionHint: To change application groups, select elements from the same group.
tags_inheritedOverrideHint: Tagging directly will override the inherited group.
tags_noTagsHelp: Tags help you organize and filter hosts, services, and other entities
tags_subtitle: Manage organization-wide tags for categorizing entities
topology_bundleEdgesHelp: Group multiple edges between the same containers into a single edge with a count badge
topology_confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete topology {name}?
topology_conflictsDetectedBody: Some entities in this diagram no longer exist. Click the badge above to review changes before updating.
topology_dontFadeEdgesHelp: Show edges at full opacity at all times
topology_elementGroupingHelp: Rules are applied top to bottom. If a node matches multiple rules, the first match takes priority.
topology_exportComplete: Export complete! Check your downloads folder.
topology_exportConfluenceDesc: Wiki markup tables for Confluence documentation
topology_exportHtmlDesc: Standalone web page for offline viewing
topology_exportMermaidDesc: Diagram for Mermaid Live Editor or GitHub
topology_exportPdfDesc: Printable document with topology map and title
topology_exportPngDesc: Raster image for presentations and reports
topology_exportSvgDesc: Vector image for documentation and embedding
topology_filtersHelp: Click items to toggle visibility. Selected items will be hidden from the visualization.
topology_hidePortsHelp: Don't show open ports next to services
topology_lockTipBody: If you want to preserve this network state as a historical record, click 'Lock' to freeze this topology instead of rebuilding.
topology_lockedInfoBody: Data can't be refreshed while this topology is locked. You can still move and resize nodes and edges, but you won't be able to make any other changes. Click the badge above to unlock and enable data refresh.
topology_multiSelectCreateGroupRebuildWa: Creating a dependency will rebuild the topology.
topology_multiSelectLockedHint: Unlock this topology to make changes.
topology_multiSelectStaleHint: Rebuild this topology to make changes.
topology_l2EmptyDescription: This view shows the physical network topology based on neighbor discovery between hosts. It will populate once hosts with discovered neighbors appear in this network.
topology_noTopologySelected: No topology selected. Create one to get started.
topology_optionDisabledRebuildRequired: Rebuild topology to change this option
topology_optionDisabledUnlockRequired: Unlock topology to change this option
topology_optionsHint: These settings apply to the entire topology
topology_removedWarning: These entities no longer exist in the network and will be removed from this diagram if you rebuild.
topology_selectParent: Select a parent to branch off of
topology_showMinimapHelp: Show a minimap overview of the topology
topology_staleDataBody: Entities have been updated, and the diagram layout may need to change to fit them.
userApiKeys_confirmBulkDelete: Are you sure you want to delete {count} API keys?
userApiKeys_confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete API key "{name}"?
userApiKeys_enableHelp: Manually enable or disable API Key. Will be auto-disabled if used after expiry date.
userApiKeys_namePlaceholder: e.g., CI/CD Pipeline, Terraform, Local Development
userApiKeys_noApiKeysSubtitle: Create API keys to access the API programmatically
userApiKeys_permissionsHelp: The maximum permission level this key can have (cannot exceed your own)
userApiKeys_subtitle: Manage your personal API keys for programmatic access
users_confirmBulkDelete: Are you sure you want to delete {count} Users?
users_confirmDeleteUser: Are you sure you want to delete this user?
users_copyFailed: Failed to copy link to clipboard
users_emailHelp: Enter the email you would like to send this invite to, or leave empty to just generate a link
users_hasAllNetworks: Users with {permissions} permissions have access to all networks.
users_inviteInstructions: Select the permissions level for the new user, then generate an invite link. They can use it to register or join your organization.
users_networkAccessHelp: Select networks this user will have access to
users_noUsersSubtitle: Users will appear here once added
users_permissionsLevelHelp: Choose the access level for the invited user
users_sensitiveLinkWarning: Anyone with this link can join your organization with {permissions} permissions. Keep it secure and only share it with people you trust.
